name: Tres Picos – circular via Tajo Negro
name_original: Tres Picos – circular por el Tajo Negro
region_intro: Jogging in Sierra Blanca offers diverse landscapes, from narrow mountain trails to scenic viewpoints. The region is characterized by its mountainous terrain, featuring limestone formations and natural features such as the Source of the Río Molinos. These routes provide opportunities to explore varied elevations and natural settings. The area is recognized as a biosphere reserve, highlighting its unique ecosystem. Best jogging routes in Sierra Blanca The most popular jogging route is Istán - Pico de la Concha Trail, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This challenging route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador de los Gitanos loop from Los Montoros, a difficult 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This route offers demanding climbs and scenic overlooks. Local runners also love the Mirador del Corzo – Mirador del Macho Montes loop from Ojén, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through varied mountain landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Jogging in Sierra Blanca is defined by narrow mountain trails, scenic viewpoints, and diverse elevations, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Sierra Blanca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 13,000 runners have used komoot to explore Sierra Blanca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sierra Blanca?
A: Sierra Blanca offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 180 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy paths to challenging mountain runs.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of jogging trails in Sierra Blanca?
A: The running trails in Sierra Blanca vary significantly in difficulty. While there are 21 easy routes suitable for beginners, the majority are moderate (109 routes) or difficult (58 routes), featuring significant elevation gains and rugged terrain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996248065/tres-picos-circular-via-tajo-negro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Picos – circular via Tajo Negro&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 5.16 km run with a 188m elevation gain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in Sierra Blanca?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are 21 easy routes that can be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous jog or walk. The region's diverse landscapes offer paths that are accessible enough for a family outing, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: The Sierra Blanca region is generally welcoming to outdoor enthusiasts and their pets. Many trails are suitable for dogs, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife. Always check local regulations for specific trails.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I discover along the jogging routes?
A: The Sierra Blanca mountains offer stunning natural features and panoramic viewpoints. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/927072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Molinos&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy breathtaking vistas from viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/892243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corzo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/923380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Lobo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also recognized as a UNESCO biosphere reserve, highlighting its unique ecosystem.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Sierra Blanca?
A: Yes, many routes in Sierra Blanca are designed as loops, offering a varied experience without retracing your steps. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996248065/tres-picos-circular-via-tajo-negro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Picos – circular via Tajo Negro&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a rewarding circular run through the landscape.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Sierra Blanca?
A: The Sierra Blanca region, particularly near the Costa Blanca, enjoys a mild Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are generally ideal for running, offering pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, though some higher elevation trails might be more challenging.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Sierra Blanca?
A: The running routes in Sierra Blanca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from narrow mountain trails to scenic viewpoints, and the challenging nature of many routes that offer rewarding experiences.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a significant challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38209441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Adelfilla loop from Altos de Xarblanca&lt;/a&gt; stand out. This route spans 17.7 km with an impressive 1,049m elevation gain, demanding very good fitness and offering an intense workout.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails?
A: The terrain in Sierra Blanca is predominantly mountainous, characterized by limestone formations and varied surfaces. You can expect a mix of narrow mountain trails, rocky paths, and some wider tracks, often with significant ascents and descents. The diverse terrain provides a dynamic and engaging running experience.

Q: Is it possible to spot wildlife while running in Sierra Blanca?
A: As a UNESCO biosphere reserve, Sierra Blanca is rich in biodiversity. While running, you may have the opportunity to spot various species of birds, and if you're lucky, some of the local mammals. Early mornings or late evenings are often the best times for wildlife viewing.

Q: Are there any less-known or 'hidden gem' trails in the area?
A: With over 180 routes, there are many trails beyond the most popular ones that offer a sense of discovery. Exploring some of the less-frequented paths can lead to unique views and a more solitary experience. Komoot's detailed maps can help you find these quieter options, often rated by local runners.
tips: The top of the Tajo Negro at 1085 masl has one of the best views towards the coast of Marbella and the interior of the Sierra de las Nieves Park. It is very close to other viewpoints that you can combine on your tour.
Beautiful views of the town of Ojén and the coast of Marbella.
